sections.paragraph: f. (from the root נוּף) waving, shaking-(a) of the hand, as a gesture of threatening, Isa 19:16.-(b) of sacrifices before Jehovah, a particular rite in offering, as to which, see הֵנִיף No. 1, d. הֲזֵה תְנוּפָה the breast of waving, i.e. offered with a particular kind of waving, Exo 29:27. Lev 7:34. זְהַב הַתְּנוּפָה Exo 38:24.-(c) i.q. tumult, Isa 30:32, מִלְחָמֹות תְּנוּפָה “wars of shaking,” tumultuous wars.
